== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6130005005802 == Commons category (P373) == * Yamaguni-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京都市右京区京北鳥居町宮ノ元1番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yamaguni-jinja}} [[JA:山国神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21019704}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Yamaguni Shrine |Image = [[File:Yamaguni-jinja, keidai.jpg|250px]]
Shrine grounds |Location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyoto City|Q34600|WD=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Ukyo Ward|Q580186|WD=Q580186|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} 1 Miyano-moto, Keihoku Torii-chō |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|10|22.3|N|135|39|20.2|E|display=ti}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto (Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto)|1=Ōkuninushi}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} era (770–780) |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = Second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Yamaguni Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yamaguni-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Yamaguni Shrine''' (やまぐにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Keihoku Torii-chō, {{ill|lt=Ukyo Ward|Q580186|WD=Q580186|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto City|Q34600|WD=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === With the relocation of the capital to {{ill|lt=Heian-kyō|Q751907|WD=Q751907|lt=the Heian capital|1=Heian-kyō}} in {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}} ({{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 13), timber was transported from {{ill|lt=Yamaguni-shō|Q21019706|WD=Q21019706|lt=the Yamaguni forestry area|1=the Yamaguni forestry area}}, and it is said that during the {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} era (770–780) the {{ill|lt=Bureau of Repairs|Q6668859|WD=Q6668859|1=Bureau of Repairs}} constructed the main hall. During its construction, {{ill|lt=Wake no Kiyomaro|Q7961005|WD=Q7961005|1=Wake no Kiyomaro}} served as the chief officiant. In {{ill|lt=1016|Q19247|WD=Q19247|1=1016}} ({{ill|lt=Chōwa|Q1194173|WD=Q1194173|1=Chōwa}} 5), {{ill|lt=Emperor Sanjō|Q357231|WD=Q357231|1=Emperor Sanjō}} bestowed upon the shrine the divine rank of Senior First Rank, designated it as a place of imperial prayer, and granted the chrysanthemum crest. This crest can still be seen on items such as the mikoshi used in the autumn festival. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|lt=Hōgen–Juei War|Q737542|WD=Q737542|lt=Genpei War|1=Genpei War}}, but was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1233|Q5421|WD=Q5421|1=1233}} ({{ill|lt=Tempuku (Japan)|Q1194452|WD=Q1194452|lt=Tempuku|1=Tenpuku}} 1) by {{ill|lt=Emperor Shijō|Q374020|WD=Q374020|1=Emperor Shijō}}. However, in {{ill|lt=1333|Q6004|WD=Q6004|1=1333}} ({{ill|lt=Genkō|Q1194556|WD=Q1194556|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} 3 / {{ill|lt=Shōkyō|Q1069086|WD=Q1069086|1=Shōkyō}} 2), it was again destroyed during the {{ill|lt=Genkō War|Q1432624|WD=Q1432624|1=Genkō War}}. In {{ill|lt=1370|Q6217|WD=Q6217|1=1370}} ({{ill|lt=Ōan|Q1146245|WD=Q1146245|1=Ōan}} 3), {{ill|lt=Hosokawa Yoriyuki|Q1139739|WD=Q1139739|1=Hosokawa Yoriyuki}}, a retainer of {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Q248381|WD=Q248381|1=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu}}, went into seclusion in {{ill|lt=Yamaguni-shō|Q21019706|WD=Q21019706|1=Yamaguni-shō}}. Upon his reinstatement three years later, he petitioned for the restoration of the shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1399|Q6422|WD=Q6422|1=1399}} ({{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 6) the shrine buildings were repaired and an imperial edict ({{ill|lt=rinji|Q11607250|WD=Q11607250|1=rinji}}) was granted. At this time, Ashikaga Yoshimitsu dedicated the “two horizontal bars within a circle” crest, which has since been used as the shrine’s emblem. The shrine was again destroyed during the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Warring States period|1=Sengoku period}}, but was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1596|Q6785|WD=Q6785|1=1596}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 1) by {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Yōzei|Q349440|WD=Q349440|1=Emperor Go-Yōzei}}.
In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6) it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in {{ill|lt=1900|Q2034|WD=Q2034|1=1900}} (Meiji 33) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} status. During this period, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fujino Itsuki|1=UNKNOWN}}, a Western Army liaison of the {{ill|lt=Yamaguni Corps|Q11467675|WD=Q11467675|1=Yamaguni Corps}}, served as chief priest. Today, Itsuki’s great-great-grandson continues to serve as the shrine’s chief priest. === Shrine grounds === Yamaguni-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall Yamaguni-jinja, shaden.jpg|Worship hall Yamaguni-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine === Annual festival === Second Sunday of October: Return Festival (offering of the Yamaguni Corps fife-and-drum march) File:Kankōsai 2019 (04).jpg| File:Kankōsai 2019 (06).jpg| File:Kankōsai 2019 (08).jpg| File:Kankōsai 2019 (09).jpg| File:Kankōsai 2019 (10).jpg| === References === Keihoku Town History Editorial Committee, *Keihoku Town History*, Keihoku Town, 1975, p. 476, Yamaguni Shrine === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Yamaguni Corps|Q11467675|WD=Q11467675|1=Yamaguni Corps}} * {{ill|lt=Jōshōkō-ji|Q11481460|WD=Q11481460|1=Jōshōkō-ji Temple}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Yamaguni-jinja}} * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/27/210/ Engishiki-listed Shrine: Yamaguni Shrine – Kyoto Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://sakigake-festa.yamaguni.com/ Yamaguni Sakigake Festa] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamaguni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamaguni Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ukyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ukyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:京北 (京都市)]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6130005005802 == Commons category (P373) == * Yamaguni-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '京都府京都市右京区京北鳥居町宮ノ元1番地', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Yamaguni-jinja}} [[JA:山国神社]] {{wikidata link|Q21019704|commons|Category:Yamaguni-jinja|ja|山国神社}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Yamaguni Shrine |Image = [[File:Yamaguni-jinja, keidai.jpg|250px]]
Shrine grounds |Location = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kyoto City|Q34600|WD=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Ukyo Ward|Q580186|WD=Q580186|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} 1 Miyano-moto, Keihoku Torii-chō |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|10|22.3|N|135|39|20.2|E|display=ti}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto (Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto)|1=Ōkuninushi}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = (Traditionally) {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} era (770–780) |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = Second Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Yamaguni Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Yamaguni-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Yamaguni Shrine''' (やまぐにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Keihoku Torii-chō, {{ill|lt=Ukyo Ward|Q580186|WD=Q580186|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto City|Q34600|WD=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === With the relocation of the capital to {{ill|lt=Heian-kyō|Q751907|WD=Q751907|lt=the Heian capital|1=Heian-kyō}} in {{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}} ({{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 13), timber was transported from {{ill|lt=Yamaguni-shō|Q21019706|WD=Q21019706|lt=the Yamaguni forestry area|1=the Yamaguni forestry area}}, and it is said that during the {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} era (770–780) the {{ill|lt=Bureau of Repairs|Q6668859|WD=Q6668859|1=Bureau of Repairs}} constructed the main hall. During its construction, {{ill|lt=Wake no Kiyomaro|Q7961005|WD=Q7961005|1=Wake no Kiyomaro}} served as the chief officiant. In {{ill|lt=1016|Q19247|WD=Q19247|1=1016}} ({{ill|lt=Chōwa|Q1194173|WD=Q1194173|1=Chōwa}} 5), {{ill|lt=Emperor Sanjō|Q357231|WD=Q357231|1=Emperor Sanjō}} bestowed upon the shrine the divine rank of Senior First Rank, designated it as a place of imperial prayer, and granted the chrysanthemum crest. This crest can still be seen on items such as the mikoshi used in the autumn festival. The shrine was destroyed during the {{ill|lt=Hōgen–Juei War|Q737542|WD=Q737542|lt=Genpei War|1=Genpei War}}, but was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1233|Q5421|WD=Q5421|1=1233}} ({{ill|lt=Tempuku (Japan)|Q1194452|WD=Q1194452|lt=Tempuku|1=Tenpuku}} 1) by {{ill|lt=Emperor Shijō|Q374020|WD=Q374020|1=Emperor Shijō}}. However, in {{ill|lt=1333|Q6004|WD=Q6004|1=1333}} ({{ill|lt=Genkō|Q1194556|WD=Q1194556|1=Genkō (1331–1334)}} 3 / {{ill|lt=Shōkyō|Q1069086|WD=Q1069086|1=Shōkyō}} 2), it was again destroyed during the {{ill|lt=Genkō War|Q1432624|WD=Q1432624|1=Genkō War}}. In {{ill|lt=1370|Q6217|WD=Q6217|1=1370}} ({{ill|lt=Ōan|Q1146245|WD=Q1146245|1=Ōan}} 3), {{ill|lt=Hosokawa Yoriyuki|Q1139739|WD=Q1139739|1=Hosokawa Yoriyuki}}, a retainer of {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Q248381|WD=Q248381|1=Ashikaga Yoshimitsu}}, went into seclusion in {{ill|lt=Yamaguni-shō|Q21019706|WD=Q21019706|1=Yamaguni-shō}}. Upon his reinstatement three years later, he petitioned for the restoration of the shrine, and in {{ill|lt=1399|Q6422|WD=Q6422|1=1399}} ({{ill|lt=Ōei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}} 6) the shrine buildings were repaired and an imperial edict ({{ill|lt=rinji|Q11607250|WD=Q11607250|1=rinji}}) was granted. At this time, Ashikaga Yoshimitsu dedicated the “two horizontal bars within a circle” crest, which has since been used as the shrine’s emblem. The shrine was again destroyed during the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Warring States period|1=Sengoku period}}, but was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1596|Q6785|WD=Q6785|1=1596}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 1) by {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Yōzei|Q349440|WD=Q349440|1=Emperor Go-Yōzei}}.
In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6) it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in {{ill|lt=1900|Q2034|WD=Q2034|1=1900}} (Meiji 33) it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} status. During this period, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Fujino Itsuki|1=UNKNOWN}}, a Western Army liaison of the {{ill|lt=Yamaguni Corps|Q11467675|WD=Q11467675|1=Yamaguni Corps}}, served as chief priest. Today, Itsuki’s great-great-grandson continues to serve as the shrine’s chief priest. === Shrine grounds === Yamaguni-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall Yamaguni-jinja, shaden.jpg|Worship hall Yamaguni-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Subsidiary shrine === Annual festival === Second Sunday of October: Return Festival (offering of the Yamaguni Corps fife-and-drum march) File:Kankōsai 2019 (04).jpg| File:Kankōsai 2019 (06).jpg| File:Kankōsai 2019 (08).jpg| File:Kankōsai 2019 (09).jpg| File:Kankōsai 2019 (10).jpg| === References === Keihoku Town History Editorial Committee, *Keihoku Town History*, Keihoku Town, 1975, p. 476, Yamaguni Shrine === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Yamaguni Corps|Q11467675|WD=Q11467675|1=Yamaguni Corps}} * {{ill|lt=Jōshōkō-ji|Q11481460|WD=Q11481460|1=Jōshōkō-ji Temple}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Yamaguni-jinja}} * [http://www.kyoto-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/27/210/ Engishiki-listed Shrine: Yamaguni Shrine – Kyoto Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://sakigake-festa.yamaguni.com/ Yamaguni Sakigake Festa] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yamaguni Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yamaguni Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Ukyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ukyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:京北 (京都市)]]
